item.title: Brisbane media personality, broadcaster, and Agro puppeteer Jamie Dunn dies aged 76
item.description: Dunn was a long-standing host of the B105 breakfast program but was known across the country as Agro — a puppet made out of a bath mat that rose to fame in 1990s.

item.title: 'Weren't planning on doing it': How Raiders pulled off match-winning play
item.description: Ethan Sanders reveals what went through his mind before he kicked a field goal to take the Raiders to a thrilling 29-28 victory over Manly in golden point.

item.title: Austin Appelbee reunited with rescuers who saved his family in WA's South West
item.description: A WA family who made global headlines when they were rescued after being swept out to sea have been reunited with the local heroes who saved them.

item.title: SA Liberals to preference One Nation over Labor
item.description: South Australia's Liberal opposition will preference One Nation over Labor at the upcoming state election as parties condemn "outrageous" comments by One Nation's Cory Bernardi.

item.title: Kyle and Jackie O superfans devastated they're not seeing eye-to-eye
item.description: An actress, electrician and real estate agent all have one thing in common – their love for Kyle and Jackie O. Here's why they've stuck by the pair through thick and thin.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: 'No way' Iran’s footballers can return home, with calls to protect them in Australia
item.description: The Iranian women's football team is facing an impossible situation. Return home, and possibly face punishment from the Islamic Republic regime for its silent protest, or stay in Australia and their families could instead suffer retribution.

item.title: Rapper-turned-politician Balen Shah defeats former prime minister in historic Nepal election
item.description: Balen Shah's Rastriya Swatantra Party is set to win a commanding share of parliamentary seats, defeating Nepal’s traditional political heavyweights.

item.title: 'Unexpected' deaths in aged care are rising. One brother demanded to see the coroner
item.description: Attila's shaky phone video of his brother lying dead in aged care documented bruises and blood. It was the start of a long campaign to understand what happened.

item.title: Hard Quiz: You can insist you're the best at trivia but I know the truth
item.description: I'm so glad you're back for another round! Just kidding, as if I'd ever say that. Let's play, HARD!

item.title: The Farrer by-election is a test for the Coalition. For voters it's an opportunity
item.description: The upcoming Farrer by-election is already being talked about as a litmus test for the new opposition leader and an ascendant One Nation. It'll also be a measure of major party politics.

item.title: Five works not to miss at Iconic Loved Unexpected at Newcastle Art Gallery
item.description: The newly expanded gallery reopens with a blockbuster exhibition, Iconic Loved Unexpected, that places local artists alongside international stars.

item.title: SA government attributed email to wrong man after his death, traumatising family
item.description: A grieving woman "feels sick" after the government releases an email it claims was written by her deceased partner praising his hospital care, after she said ambulance ramping had denied him dignity in his final hours.

item.title: All-ages heavy metal music events in Sydney not dead as young people keep headbanging
item.description: In community halls across Western Sydney young people are headbanging, crowdsurfing and forming mosh pits.

item.title: SA election promises ramped down as ambulance crisis proves hard to solve
item.description: With just two weeks to go until the state election, Labor has steered away from ambitious promises after difficulty meeting its pledge to fix the ambulance ramping crisis last election — one of the few points where the opposition's barbs are piercing.

item.title: Australia hoping Alyssa Healy bats one last time in Test farewell against India in Perth
item.description: Alyssa Healy is being encouraged to promote herself up the batting order, as the Australia captain prepares for the final act of her international career.

item.title: Calls for Elizabeth Woolcock to be posthumously pardoned more than 150 years after her execution
item.description: Elizabeth Woolcock is recognised as the only woman ever to be hanged in SA. But researchers believe she was sent to the gallows in 1873 an innocent woman.

item.title: Veil worn by 27 brides, and their wedding photographs form National Library display celebrating family history
item.description: What started out as a wartime necessity was passed down from generation to generation, becoming a family relic now on display in the National Library.

item.title: Female farmers feel overlooked, under-represented due to 'patchy progress'
item.description: After 30 years of "patchy progress", women in agriculture are striving for more recognition and understanding of their crucial role on the land.

item.title: Katherine experiences worst flooding in 28 years as remote NT communities evacuated
item.description: Katherine experiences its worst flooding in 28 years, with several Territorians rescued by helicopter this weekend after they were stranded in a nearby remote community.

item.title: Mackay SLSC Surf Boat Rowing
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: City of Hobart shipwreck officially found off Gippsland after 150 years
item.description: A renewable energy company's search for an offshore wind farm site leads divers to the wreckage of the City of Hobart, which sank nearly 150 years ago off Victoria's east coast.

item.title: Celebrated for its sea life and pristine beaches, Shark Bay is preserved by those who love it
item.description: This tiny community is enduring the ultimate balancing act, keeping hordes of tourists happy, mitigating the impacts of climate change and protecting their World Heritage-listed home. Lisa Millar explores how Shark Bay not only survives, but thrives.

item.title: Government considering request for military assistance from Gulf States attacked by Iran
item.description: Foreign Affairs Minister Penny Wong says the government is considering requests for military assistance from Gulf countries targeted by Iranian attacks.

item.title: Free dance classes run by 12yo champ keep girls on their toes in Armidale
item.description: Financial stress means some families cannot afford their children's dance classes, so Armidale tween Annabelle has started a free dance school to keep their dreams alive.

item.title: The K-pop concert that sparked an online war across Asia
item.description: Criticism of Korean fans bringing professional cameras into a Korean boy band concert in Malaysia, against local rules, spiralled into an online war from Vietnam to Indonesia.

item.title: Dolphins and a seal found dead on Eyre Peninsula beaches
item.description: Large, dead marine animals found on Eyre Peninsula beaches spark concern among locals.

item.title: Menopause isn't easy, but for women living in Pilbara heat becomes 'stress multiplier'
item.description: Pilbara woman Bree Maher spent nearly a year trying to find the right support for her menopause symptoms. She said the heat of northern Western Australia amplified her feelings of isolation.

item.title: How to improve the energy efficiency in older Australian homes
item.description: Owners of older homes are being urged to have their energy efficiency assessed, with experts saying improvements will not only make them more comfortable, but help them save money as well.

item.title: Cane toads bred in WA's Kimberley protect goannas from fatal consequences
item.description: Field work is underway near Broome where researchers are deliberately breeding the invasive cane toad as part of a strategy to protect native wildlife.

item.title: Tumut is hoping to become the giant pumpkin capital of Australia
item.description: Deep in the NSW Snowy Mountains, these heavyweights of the veggie patch are determined to grow the biggest pumpkins in the country.

item.title: Are couples happier sleeping separately?
item.description: Despite many people like Hannah finding a "sleep divorce" improves their sleep — and their relationship — a "pervasive stigma" about not sharing a bed persists.

item.title: Crocodile crossing the road during NT flood event
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: SA government's cancer patient email blunder was 'completely unacceptable', premier says
item.description: Peter Malinauskas says his government made an "unacceptable" error in releasing an email it claimed was from a terminal cancer patient who later died, but that turned out to be from a different man with the same name.

item.title: Australia and Canada share similar values. Remind us what they are again
item.description: When Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney visited Australia last week, he had a message about Australia's and Canada's shared values. He said they were worth defending and could be used to build a more prosperous future.

item.title: Bennett pleads with fans to stay off the field after Johnston's record try
item.description: The Rabbitohs take down the Dolphins in a 70-point clash at Lang Park, with South Sydney's Alex Johnston scoring his 211th career try to move within one of Ken Irvine's record.

item.title: Healy bids farewell as Australia seals crushing Test win over India
item.description: Alyssa Healy signs off her illustrious career in style as Australia wipes away India to claim an easy win in Perth.

item.title: Western Sydney earmarked as potential location for state's second film studio
item.description: The NSW government has earmarked three areas in Western Sydney as possible locations for the state's second film studio.

item.title: Nationals select former military commander Brad Robertson to contest Farrer by-election
item.description: The National Party's candidate for the Farrer by-election, Brad Robertson, says the people of Farrer have been "waiting" for the Nationals to come back.

item.title: Premier's boat trip passed into sanctuary zone while towing lures, RecFish SA concedes
item.description: An election campaign boat trip involving the SA premier has faced criticism after the state's peak recreational fishing body — which says it organised the trip — admitted it breached marine regulations by towing lures in a sanctuary zone.

item.title: Collingwood claims spiteful MCG thriller against St Kilda
item.description: St Kilda's star recruits aren't enough to overcome Collingwood in a thriller at the MCG to cap off Opening Round.

item.title: Matildas finish second in Asian Cup group after pulsating draw with South Korea
item.description: Alanna Kennedy nets a last-gasp equaliser, but it's not enough for the Matildas. They finish second in Group A after a pulsating 3-3 draw with Korea Republic in Sydney.

item.title: When will schools and shops reopen? How Katherine and surrounds will move through flood recovery
item.description: Wondering if schools in the Top End will be open tomorrow? Not sure what the situation is with flood evacuation centres? Looking for the latest information on natural disaster relief payments? We've answered all your NT flood-related questions here.

item.title: Floodwaters flowing down Katherine Gorge quickly
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Oil markets can't bank on deal maker Donald Trump in the Middle East
item.description: Investors seem calm, relying on Donald Trump's reputation as a deal maker but the reality is this conflict could be long and destabilising.

item.title: Labor acutely aware of risks in backing US-Israeli attacks on Iran
item.description: The Albanese government will need to be explicit about what it is doing in the Middle East or risk a growing concern that it is being drawn into another war.

item.title: Courtney Moir's Muster Dogs win fuels passion for dog handling, motherhood
item.description: After swapping corporate city life for her family's remote seventh-generation farm, Courtney Moir has claimed the top spot in this year's season of Muster Dogs.

item.title: This week in history quiz, March 9: Can you pass the test?
item.description: A royal family photo, Buffy the Vampire Slayer and Australia's first inland city.

item.title: What is a career break and how do you ask for one?
item.description: Whether it’s for a holiday, additional study or a exploring a new field of work, experts say a career break can have many benefits.

item.title: Dubai-based Australian companies caught in crossfire as tensions escalate in Middle East
item.description: Escalating geopolitical tensions mean Australians operating businesses in the Middle East have had to make quick changes to their daily operations.

item.title: Russia a 'big winner' in oil crisis sparked by Middle East war and Strait of Hormuz closure
item.description: Russia has condemned the US and Israeli strikes on Iran, but its economy and in turn war efforts could be boosted thanks the oil crisis created from the war.

item.title: What will happen to my son when I die?
item.description: Deirdre Croft has dedicated 45 years to caring and advocating for her intellectually disabled son, Richard. Each passing day brings more pressure to secure his future when she's gone. It's a distressing scenario thousands of Australian parents face.

item.title: Israel expands Iran strikes as Tehran moves to name new supreme leader
item.description: Israeli forces expand their bombardment of Iran, striking fuel depots near Tehran as the country moves closer to naming a new supreme leader.

item.title: EMDR mental health therapy helped Katie heal, but her court case was discontinued
item.description: Katie Bird sought psychological help for intrusive memories related to alleged sexual abuse. Her court case was discontinued because she underwent eye movement desensitisation and reprocessing (EMDR) therapy.

item.title: South Australian voters want more transport options, such as Adelaide Hills rail
item.description: As more houses are being built in Adelaide's outer suburbs, more public transport is something on the minds of South Australian voters, in both the regions and the city.

item.title: Canberra Cavalry to learn fate after withdrawing from league due to 'unsustainable' financial situation
item.description: The Canberra Cavalry will learn later this month whether it can rejoin the Australian Baseball League after withdrawing from the 2025-26 season citing financial problems.

item.title: Australia sitting on over $1 billion of unused medical research funding as scientists abandon the field
item.description: Researchers say securing funding has become increasingly tough, with many left with no choice but to spend a disproportionate amount of time applying for grants.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Frustrated Matildas exposed by South Korea, but say long road won't derail them
item.description: In the moments that mattered, the Matildas lacked composure, while South Korea made them pay. They'll now have to do it the hard way if they're going to go all the way at the Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: India dominates New Zealand in the T20 World Cup final to claim back-to-back titles
item.description: India is sent in to bat in the T20 World Cup final and dominates from start to finish to claim its third title.

item.title: Regional patient 'effectively ramped' for almost three days waiting for care
item.description: When Riverland farmer Mick Evans had a suspected heart attack last year, he was stranded at a regional hospital for almost three days after an airlift request was continually cancelled and there were no available beds at the Royal Adelaide Hospital.

item.title: New F1 regulations debut at Australian Grand Prix, with mixed reviews from the paddock
item.description: Formula 1's new era began with a dramatic, chaotic, and compelling grand prix in Melbourne. While views on the regulations are mixed, fans will likely be the ones to decide if it is a success or failure.

item.title: Campers flooded in at Byfield following heavy rains
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Phosphate Hill fertiliser plant sold to Mayfair, ensuring continued operations at copper smelter
item.description: A major outback mining asset near Mount Isa has been sold to a Queensland-based company for up to $100 million, potentially securing the town's copper production.

item.title: Why Bridget Jones's Diary still resonates with readers 30 years later
item.description: When Oxford-educated Helen Fielding introduced readers to Bridget Jones in her newspaper column, in her own words, she was "quite embarrassed". Three decades later, the book has sold more than 15 million copies worldwide.

item.title: Police speaking with 'a number of witnesses' following alleged campsite stabbing near Tamworth
item.description: Police allege an altercation between two men has ended in a fatal stabbing at a campsite near Tamworth.

item.title: Socceroos keeper Joe Gauci stars as Port Vale downs Sunderland in FA Cup boilover
item.description: Socceroos keeper Joe Gauci and Aussie manager Jon Brady guide Port Vale past Sunderland into the FA Cup quarters for the first time in 72 years.

item.title: Where have Israel and the US been striking, and where has Iran retaliated?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Food supply lines open as Katherine and Daly River under emergency flood warning
item.description: While Katherine remains under an emergency flood warning, water levels are now receding and food supply lines are opening up.

item.title: Who is Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei?
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's son Mojtaba, a hardline, anti-Western mid-ranking cleric, has been chosen to succeed his late father as Iran's supreme leader.

item.title: How Donald Trump and Pete Hegseth are selling the Iran war to Americans
item.description: The US administration's unapologetic stance on the war has come with a lot of bravado, but there are signs the American public is not convinced.

item.title: Zac Lomax signs two-year Super Rugby deal in major blow to NRL
item.description: Zac Lomax signs a deal with Western Force and Rugby Australia in a major blow to the NRL.

item.title: SA health minister 'very sorry' for leak to media of wrong patient's email
item.description: South Australia's health minister apologises "unreservedly" for the deliberate leak of an email that the government said was from an Adelaide cancer patient who died in 2025, but turned out to be from a different person with the same name.

item.title: Tasmania flags new laws to stop 'good character' evidence as mitigating factor for serious offending
item.description: Tasmania will introduce new laws to stop the use of "good character" references by serious offenders. Advocates say the state has joined a broader national shift to support victim-survivors.

item.title: Masked German football fan unplugs VAR monitor in protest during Bundesliga 2 game
item.description: Frustrated football fans take their anger at VAR to the next level, with a masked supporter pulling the plug on a replay monitor during a Bundesliga 2 game.

item.title: Desperation for Iran's women's footballers, as coach says 'we are eager to come back to Iran'
item.description: There is an increasing sense of urgency among members of the Iranian Australian community who are desperate to hear from the women's team before they leave Australia, and fear for their safety if they return to Iran.

item.title: Former NSW Liberal MP Rory Amon cleared of majority of charges in sexual assault trial
item.description: A jury has cleared former NSW Liberal MP Rory Amon of the majority of charges he faced over the alleged sexual assault of a 13-year-old boy he met through a hook-up app in 2017.

item.title: Tehranian residents shouting death to the new ayatollah
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle confirm Australian visit in April
item.description: The visit will mark seven years since they last touched down in the country for their successful tour as the newlywed Duke and Duchess of Sussex.

item.title: Essendon coach Brad Scott says his club has moved on from Zach Merrett saga as Bombers set to face Hawthorn
item.description: Former Essendon captain Zach Merrett is the centre of attention in the build-up to a round-one blockbuster against the club he attempted to join in the off-season.

item.title: Bywong man who dragged conscious kangaroo behind car fails bid to reduce sentence
item.description: A Bywong man's attempt to have his sentence for aggravated animal cruelty reduced, after dragging a conscious kangaroo behind his car last year, is dismissed in the Queanbeyan Local Court.

item.title: Cleaning job offer led to woman's arrest at Calderwood cannabis farm, court hears
item.description: A judge hears a 32-year-old Vietnamese woman arrested at a cannabis farm south of Wollongong had travelled to the property on the promise of a lucrative cleaning job.

item.title: Five recipes to make lunchtime leftovers a breeze
item.description: Looking to make lunchtime super simple and satisfying? Try these five dishes.

item.title: Territory Labor's Ed Smelt wins Nightcliff by-election, taking the Greens' only NT seat
item.description: A check count conducted by the Northern Territory Electoral Commission has slightly increased Ed Smelt's lead to 158 votes for Labor, over the Greens' Suki Dorras-Walker.

item.title: Lebanon mass displacement causes anxiety for diaspora in Australia
item.description: The Lebanese government says more than 500,000 people have been displaced since the Israeli military issued evacuation warnings for villages in the country's south.

item.title: Defying Donald Trump and stepping out of the shadows, Mojtaba Khamenei ascends
item.description: US President Donald Trump said the worst-case scenario in Iran would be if Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's successor were "as bad as the previous person". In many ways, the appointment of his son, Mojtaba Khamenei, delivers him that very outcome.

item.title: Bondi Beach gunman Naveed Akram seeks order to suppress family's identity over safety concerns
item.description: Naveed Akram faces 59 charges, including 15 counts of murder and one of committing a terrorist attack, over the shooting on December 14.

item.title: Saltwater crocodile found in dam hundreds of kilometres from normal habitat
item.description: Authorities say they will rehome a 2.3 metre saltwater crocodile that was found in a dam near Gatton, hundreds of kilometres from its usual habitat.

item.title: NAB warns rising petrol prices could drive inflation above 5 per cent
item.description: More pain could be coming from motorists if economists' predictions about the impact of rising fuel prices on inflation prove correct.

item.title: Farmers stockpile diesel as Middle East conflict prompts fuel supply fears
item.description: Farmers say consumers could soon feel the impact of fuel shortages and soaring prices at the checkout, as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupts global supply chains.

item.title: As the Iran war rages on, historic oil price surge sends share markets into a spin
item.description: For more than a year, traders have been pushing markets higher on the hopes of an AI-powered economic miracle and the notion of TACO — Trump Always Chickens Out. Now it looks like the penny has dropped.

item.title: Man charged with murder after alleged assault and robbery in Perth's CBD
item.description: Police charge a 37-year-old man with murder after he allegedly attacked a 62-year-old man in Perth's CBD, causing him to fall and strike his head on the pavement.

item.title: Multiple explosions in Khorramabad, Iran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Fire on the streets of Tehran after explosions at oil depot
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Big Red Bash site will not be dry enough to hold festival in July, search for new venue
item.description: The event is still four months away, but organisers say the site will not be sufficiently dry in time.

item.title: Former US diplomat says efforts to topple Iran's leadership may backfire and strengthen regime
item.description: A former top US diplomat says her country's efforts to topple Iran's leadership risks destabilising the region and strengthening the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.

item.title: Australian men sentenced in Indonesian court for Bali murder of Zivan Radmanovic
item.description: An Indonesian court has jailed two Australian men for 16 years for the murder of Zivan Radmanovic, 32, in June last year.

item.title: Monday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Court rules WA Housing Authority does not owe consumer guarantee to tenants
item.description: Perth Magistrates Court has ruled against a First Nations woman's challenge after a long legal battle over housing neglect in WA's Pilbara region.

item.title: War in Middle East enters second week
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Katherine locals survey damage from major flooding as volunteer 'army' rallies to help
item.description: An "army" of volunteers has hit the streets of Katherine to help residents worst affected by the weekend's flooding, as they survey the damage from near-record river rises.

item.title: Emergency services prioritised as some regional petrol stations ration fuel
item.description: Petrol stations in some WA towns begin restricting petrol sales to emergency services only, as impacts from the Iran war hit the state.

item.title: Labor MP warns against 'dangerous' linking of Israeli government to antisemitism
item.description: Josh Burns also acknowledged the government had not always acted fast enough to deal with antisemitism and said it was necessary to be "humble" in the wake of the Bondi terror attack.

item.title: Five Iranian football players granted asylum amid fears of persecution
item.description: Five members of the Iranian women's soccer team, who were labelled "traitors" on Iranian state TV, have been granted asylum after fears they would be targeted by the Iranian regime if they returned home.

item.title: Prosecutors expand Swiss ski bar fatal fire probe to include mayor, documents show
item.description: Swiss prosecutors have added the mayor to those under criminal investigation for a fatal New Years Eve ski resort fire that killed 41 people.

item.title: Homemade bomb at New York anti-Islam rally near Zohran Mamdani's residence investigated as 'act of ISIS-inspired terrorism'
item.description: A homemade bomb thrown near an anti-Islam protest in New York at the weekend is being investigated as an Islamic State-inspired act of "terrorism," police say.

item.title: Hegseth signals 'most intense day' for US strikes on Iran, seven Iranian footballers seek asylum — as it happened
item.description: Video captured by a protester shows an Iranian footballer being led, seemingly reluctantly, by her wrist by a team member onto a bus headed for the airport, with the opposition calling the footage "concerning". Follow live.

item.title: Farmers, community groups call for wild deer to be labelled a pest as population balloons
item.description: The Tasmanian government asked the public what it thought about how wild fallow deer were being managed. It received hundreds of submissions calling for deer to be labelled a pest and for improved zoning and hunting permits.

item.title: The rise of cowboy core: What's behind it and what does it mean for Australian brands?
item.description: From streetwear to festival fits, country fashion has become mainstream in Australian cities and heritage brands are reaping the benefits.

item.title: The Bride! Is a mismatched metatextual movie marriage
item.description: The Bride!, a modern retelling of The Bride of Frankenstein, takes massive swings in terms of performances, plotting and subtext, but not all of its gambles pay off.

item.title: Higher oil and fuel prices hit road transport industry that moves 'everything you buy in shops'
item.description: Increasing fuel prices are putting pressure on the road transport industry, with the potential to impact the price of everything from furniture to food.

item.title: ABC Shakespeare quiz: How well doest thou know his characters and quotes? Testeth thineself hither
item.description: A quiz by any other word would smell as sweet, or something, so have a crack at our 10 questions on the works of William Shakespeare.

item.title: Australian Border Force intercepts 150,000 units of melatonin amid crackdown on unregulated products
item.description: Australian Border Force officials have intercepted more than 150,000 units of melatonin in four months, after authorities warned they would crack down on unregulated products.

item.title: Brisbane Lions to fight Harris Andrews and Darcy Gardiner bans at AFL tribunal
item.description: Brisbane begins its season with two pivotal cases at the AFL tribunal in a bid to not have its defensive stocks completely depleted heading into the first month.

item.title: How the 'tough guy' of South-East Asia is reshaping Australia's largest neighbour
item.description: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto's big ambitions on the world stage and at home have alarmed his critics.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Matildas learn quarterfinal opponent as North Korea sensationally protests offside call
item.description: The Matildas will face North Korea in the quarterfinals of the Women's Asian Cup, after the North Koreans go down 2-1 to China in controversial circumstances at a soaking Western Sydney Stadium.

item.title: Current and former Liberals are quietly behind two 'grassroots' childcare campaigns
item.description: The childcare groups say they are the voices for everyday parents. What they do not mention is that they are supported by a network of current and former Liberals.

item.title: Decline in GP home visits drives vulnerable people into care homes
item.description: The number of GP home visits has declined by 85 per cent in Australia over the past three decades, which doctors say is driving vulnerable people into residential care and hospital prematurely.

item.title: Scientists call for urgent funding as hundreds of CSIRO job cuts loom
item.description: Scientists are calling on the Australian government to push back on a plan to scrap hundreds of jobs at the nation's science agency, as they await the release of further details this week.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Laura Tingle
item.description: The ABC's global affairs editor Laura Tingle is in Dubai and will provide daily updates on the Middle East war.

item.title: International students blindsided by federal government's doubling of temporary graduate visa cost
item.description: The Department of Home Affairs has doubled the price of a post-study work visa, leaving "tens of thousands" of graduates out of pocket.

item.title: Michael Milton overcome with emotion after ending two-decade hiatus by competing at 2026 Paralympics
item.description: Michael Milton is overcome with emotion after making his Paralympics return, but is less than impressed by his actual performance on the slopes.

item.title: Patient information should not be used as 'political tool', peak doctors body says
item.description: The Australian Medical Association says politicians should not be using non-consensual patient information to make a point about the health system.

item.title: Sixth case of flesh-eating Buruli ulcer appears on NSW South Coast
item.description: The sixth case of a flesh-eating Buruli ulcer has been detected on the NSW South Coast.

item.title: Residents in four Bundaberg suburbs urged to 'leave immediately' amid major flood warning for Burnett River
item.description: Residents in four Bundaberg suburbs have been issued with an emergency warning to "leave immediately" ahead of major flooding on the Burnett River, which runs through the city.

item.title: Sharing of wrong patient's email provides rare public glimpse into the cunning world of political spin
item.description: It is common for media advisors to provide reporters with background information in an effort to shape the way a story is reported, but the practice backfired spectacularly when the SA government shared an email from the wrong patient.

item.title: Dubai's influencers have a new rule: Don't mention the war
item.description: As missiles and drones began lighting up the sky over Dubai, many of the city's social media personalities started posting about it. Then the government stepped in.

item.title: Queensland calls on Energy Minister Chris Bowen for update on government's fuel security plan
item.description: Queensland Energy Minister David Janetzki has written to federal counterpart Chris Bowen warning of major impacts on daily life.

item.title: Jai Opetaia says he is 'the only one suffering' after being stripped of IBF belt yet again
item.description: Jai Opetaia isn't giving up his dream of unification despite being caught in the middle of an administrative slugfest.

item.title: ASX closes 1pc higher, petrol prices up 17c a litre in last week — as it happened
item.description: The Australian market closed 1 per cent higher, regaining some of yesterday's losses. Catch up on the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

item.title: Inside the underground custody cells at the NT's busiest courthouse
item.description: The ABC has been given a glimpse inside the custody cells at the Darwin Local Court that hold a revolving door of men and women arrested every week.

item.title: Australia to provide military support to Gulf states attacked by Iran
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese confirms the Australian Defence Force will deploy an E-7A Wedgetail aircraft to the Gulf to help protect and defend the region, providing long-range reconnaissance activity.

item.title: Jungle King boat on Saxon Reef
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Rainforest river cruise boat found on Great Barrier Reef after flash flood
item.description: Operators in the world heritage Daintree Rainforest are anxious about lost business after tour boats and ferries were damaged in floods ahead of the Easter holidays.

item.title: Residents raise concerns about housing development after unprecedented floods
item.description: Residents of a Central Victorian town are frustrated after being hit with two unprecedented flooding events in a couple of days.

item.title: Father facing murder charge over stabbing death of son in Maudsland on Gold Coast
item.description: A 54-year-old man has been arrested and charged with one count of murder and domestic violence following the death of his son at a Maudsland home.

item.title: Australia eliminated from World Baseball Classic despite Travis Bazzana's heroics against South Korea
item.description: Australia's incredible campaign at the World Baseball Classic comes to a heartbreaking end after narrowly failing to qualify for the next stage.

item.title: Boil water alert issued, tap pressure to be reduced amid 'unprecedented' flooding at Darwin's main drinking supply
item.description: A boil water alert has been issued and tap pressure will be reduced as water authorities grapple with "unprecedented" flooding at Darwin River Dam.

item.title: Violent brawl between Cruzeiro and Atletico Mineiro in Brazilian league sees 23 players red-carded
item.description: A brawl which featured players, coaches and security staff in Brazil's soccer league has seen 23 players red-carded afterwards.

item.title: Slaying the AI dragon and appealing for the human touch in performance in Perth Festival debut
item.description: Never mind artificial intelligence, this is dragon intelligence. A new play commissioned for the Perth Festival explores the importance of human creativity, even if AI can help with an unfinished novel.

item.title: Far West NSW graziers who went from drought to flooding rains begin clean-up and search for stock
item.description: As water begins to recede after record-breaking rains, outback New South Wales graziers are assessing the damage and searching for stock.

item.title: Rain blankets properties in far west NSW
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sheep stuck in flood waters
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Labor to pass super tax changes with support of Greens
item.description: The legislation comes as speculation builds that the federal government will pursue more ambitious tax changes in the May budget.

item.title: Bundaberg residents told to 'leave immediately' as floodwaters rise, bridges to shut — as it happened
item.description: A major flood is threatening Bundaberg after days of rain spilled from dams and set rivers on the rise across Queensland.

item.title: NBA cancels Atlanta Hawks's plan to honour local strip club Magic City with promotion night
item.description: The Atlanta Hawks will no longer go ahead with its plans to promote Magic City, a strip club that is famous for adult entertainment and chicken wings.

item.title: Australian women's cricket captain Sophie Molineux returns for tour of West Indies
item.description: Australia's women's cricket team receives a boost for the West Indies series, with Sophie Molineux expected to return from her back injury.

item.title: Hungary seizes money and gold in deepening diplomatic spat with Ukraine
item.description: Hungary has seized around $115 million and gold from a Ukrainian bank transfer and temporarily detained seven Ukrainians, as Budapest pressures Kyiv to restart oil shipments from Russia.

item.title: US and Arab states turn to Ukraine for help against Iranian drone attacks
item.description: Ukraine is called upon to provide drone defence expertise to the US and Gulf states as the region struggles to counter swarms of Iranian attack drones.

item.title: Canberra man charged with manslaughter after allegedly neglecting elderly mother
item.description: A Canberra man is facing a manslaughter charge after allegedly neglecting his elderly mother who was found living in extremely poor conditions before her death.

item.title: Iranian soccer players escape minders and secure asylum in Australia
item.description: Australian officials waited on the sidelines of the Iranian football team's final game in the Gold Coast on Sunday, signalling to players help was available if they wanted to talk. The government has granted asylum to five athletes who escaped from their hotel with assistance from Australian Federal Police.

item.title: Why Alex Johnston's pursuit of the all-time try-scoring record is a wonder half a century in the making
item.description: As Alex Johnston moves to the edge of forever in his chase of the all-time try-scoring record, rugby league must brace itself for a chance that only comes around once or twice a century.

item.title: Burwood North rezoning around new metro station could see thousands of new homes
item.description: The NSW government on Tuesday unveiled plans to rezone the 113-hectare Burwood North precinct, positioning it as a residential hub between Sydney's CBD and Parramatta.

item.title: Middle East conflict hiking fertiliser costs for Australian farmers but what does it mean for grocery prices?
item.description: The Middle East conflict is driving up the cost of an essential fertiliser for Australian farmers, but there are other factors more likely to affect grocery prices.

item.title: Five things I do (as a therapist) when anxiety takes over
item.description: No-one is harder on me than me. Anxiety thrives on this. Along the way, I've built a toolkit that helps me regulate, reconnect and come back to myself.

item.title: Teenager who killed Vyleen White in Redbank Plains car park robbery appeals sentence
item.description: The 17-year-old boy, who cannot legally be named, pleaded guilty to Vyleen White's murder and several other offences last year.

item.title: Targo Street in Bundaberg
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Aerial footage shows the Paradise Dam — west of Bundaberg — spilling as the Burnett River continues to rise.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Who are the Iranian Women's Asian Cup players granted asylum by Australia?
item.description: Australia has granted five Iranian players asylum after their Women's Asian Cup campaign. So, who are they?

item.title: Court dismisses former MP Annabel Digance's $2.3m lawsuit against SA Premier Peter Malinauskas
item.description: South Australia's Supreme Court has thrown out former MP Annabel Digance's $2.3 million lawsuit against the state and Premier Peter Malinauskas, but found it was still open for her to pursue complaints about the conduct of SA Police.

item.title: Heavy rainfall continues to threaten Katherine's flood recovery as rivers swell in Beswick, Daly River
item.description: More rain could see river levels rise even further, threatening remote NT communities, while the clean-up has started in Katherine and businesses reopen.

item.title: Push for investigation into safety, durability of Great Western Highway's historic Convict Bridge
item.description: The Blue Mountains community warned the NSW government the Great Western Highway's historic Convict Bridge could not withstand modern day traffic prior to its closure last week.

item.title: What is a Wedgetail? And why is Australia sending missiles to the Iran conflict?
item.description: One of Australia's most sophisticated military surveillance planes will be deployed to the Middle East, along with a crew of about 85 defence personnel to operate it.

item.title: Aid workers search residential area in Resalat, Tehran after strike
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Teenager's family say Aidan Becker showed 'extraordinary courage' during alleged Mernda attack
item.description: The family of a 14-year-old boy allegedly attacked in Mernda last week said Aidan Becker, who was fatally stabbed trying to protect the teenager, showed "extraordinary courage and compassion in risking his life to save our son".

item.title: NSW premier unsure when troubled M6 will open after sinkhole issues
item.description: NSW Premier Chris Minns says he does not know if the troubled M6 motorway will open while he is still in power.

item.title: New Mexico prosecutors launch search of Jeffrey Epstein's secluded former Zorro Ranch
item.description: New Mexico state investigators search a secluded ranch where financier and sex offender Jeffrey Epstein once entertained guests amid allegations that the property may have been used for sexual abuse and sex trafficking of young women.

item.title: Indigenous artworks taken on 5,000km road trip from the Pilbara to Biennale of Sydney
item.description: From the Pilbara to Penrith, an Indigenous WA community has taken its prized cultural and artistic possessions across the country for the Biennale of Sydney.

item.title: Darwin issued a boil water alert, here's what you need to know
item.description: A boil water alert has been issued for Darwin, in addition to Katherine, Beswick and Tindal. The alert comes after issues with the pump station at Darwin's main dam. Here's what you need to know about your water.

item.title: New laws to temporarily ban people coming to Australia from designated countries
item.description: Visitors from some countries could be temporarily blocked from Australia by a federal government concerned about a wave of people overstaying their visas due to the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Ballarat Muslims traumatised after right-wing attack on community dinner
item.description: A Muslim community in Ballarat is left traumatised after a man barged into a community dinner allegedly throwing punches and yelling abuse.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands insists he is not in breach of $100m contract over on-air fight with co-host Jackie O
item.description: In his first statement since he was ordered off the air after a blow-up with co-host Jackie "O" Henderson, Sandilands calls on ARN Media, owner of KIIS FM, to "honour our agreement" and let him return to the Kyle and Jackie O Show.

item.title: Zac Lomax admits he is a rugby union 'novice', but has no regrets about end to NRL career
item.description: Rugby recruit Zac Lomax knows he has a long way to go to fulfil his dream of becoming a dual international and playing for the Wallabies at the 2027 World Cup.

item.title: Linda Reynolds and Commonwealth enter mediation in bid to resolve federal court dispute
item.description: The Federal Court orders mediation between former WA senator Linda Reynolds and the Commonwealth in her legal claim over its conduct during a financial settlement with Brittany Higgins.

item.title: Petrol prices surge across Asia, prompting rationing and price caps
item.description: From closing schools to daily limits, the surging cost of oil prompts countries across Asia to take action to ensure fuel supply and keep prices in check.

item.title: Emotional David Littleproud steps down as leader of the National Party
item.description: The Queenslander cried as he spoke to reporters, saying it had been a "pretty rough" time since the May election.

item.title: How a war in the Middle East caused Australia's sudden fuel price hikes
item.description: If you've topped up on fuel this week, you likely would have noticed prices at the bowser have jumped a fair bit. Here's a quick breakdown on why that happened.

item.title: The government is not speaking plainly about joining the war effort in the Middle East
item.description: It's a reasonable question for Australians to ask given the government is sending a sophisticated reconnaissance plane — crewed by dozens of military personnel — to the Middle East. And legal experts say the answer is yes.

item.title: Driver in fatal Daylesford crash questioned over diabetes management
item.description: The driver who was behind the wheel of a car that ploughed into a group of people outside a central Victorian pub has detailed his sorrow over the crash.

item.title: Migration agent shares details of secret operation to get Iranian football asylum seekers alone
item.description: After the increasing urgency over the fate of Iran's women's football team over the past few days, the breakthrough came last night, when five players were able to seek independent advice.

item.title: Bundaberg residents evacuate homes as major flooding nears its peak
item.description: Bundaberg residents are evacuating their homes and businesses as the city faces its worst flood in more than a decade.

item.title: Iranian football player led onto bus from hotel
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Lachlan Bowles was wearing Nazi armband during Kellerberrin shooting, inquest hears
item.description: A man who fatally shot his colleague at a Wheatbelt grain silo before turning the weapon on himself had undiagnosed mental health issues and was exploring extremist ideology, an inquest hears.

item.title: Australian footballer Barry Cable on trial for alleged sexual abuse of girl in 1960s
item.description: Ex-Australian football Hall of Famer Barry Cable picked up a girl from an orphanage in Perth and sexually abused her multiple times in the 1960s, a court has been told.

item.title: Public health patient demands apology after private health info given to media
item.description: Monica Wohlstadt is the second person in a matter of days to raise concerns about how the SA health minister's office used personal patient information to "background" journalists ahead of the publication of stories.

item.title: Tomahawk missile strikes Iranian school
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How Donald Trump's latest Iran war update fits into the US administration's shifting statements
item.description: US President Donald Trump has said the war in Iran could be over "very soon", but has still not offered a definitive end date for the conflict.

item.title: Electoral review proposes scrapping two Queensland seats, changing boundaries of dozens more
item.description: The first review into Queensland's electoral boundaries since 2017 has recommended for two electorates to be abolished, two more created, and almost 50 changed.

item.title: Petrol prices leap to national average of 198 cents per litre, new data shows
item.description: New data has confirmed a significant rise in fuel costs, with the national average petrol price increasing 17 cents over the week to Sunday, to 198 cents a litre, as analysts warn there's more room for it to rise.

item.title: Super Netball's Kiwi influx leaves sour taste for diehard Australian fans
item.description: Nine Kiwi players will feature in Super Netball for 2026, after the New Zealand governing body relaxed its national selection policy. But their influx has left a sour taste in the mouth of rusted-on Australian Diamonds fans.

item.title: WA Police officer-in-charge captures crocodile from Kimberley backyard.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Fitzroy Crossing's top cop wrangles Kimberley crocodile in backyard
item.description: Fitzroy Crossing police officer-in-charge Mark Howes says no-one else was available to help remove a freshwater crocodile from a local backyard, "so I thought I'd have a crack".

item.title: Hobart's Macquarie Point stadium not expected to host AFL games until 2031
item.description: When the $1.13 billion stadium was approved last year, it was hoped the Tasmania Devils would begin playing AFL games there from 2029. The head of the corporation tasked with delivering the stadium says her expectations are set a little bit later.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Search for missing Port Pirie woman extends to Brighton
item.description: South Australian police expand their search for "high-risk missing person" Patricia Ewens, who has not been seen for almost three weeks.

item.title: US releases footage of missile launch during Iran conflict
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Five Iranian women’s soccer players granted humanitarian visas
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Australia joins the war effort in the Middle East.
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Allegations of discrimination against First Nations staff in ACT Public Service referred to independent watchdog
item.description: Allegations of systemic discrimination against First Nations staff in the ACT Public Service have been referred to the independent watchdog.

item.title: Is there a crisis in our mental health system?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Seven members of Iran’s women’s football team have sought asylum, ABC understands
item.description: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke, who was at the airport, said border force officials made multiple representations to all of the team before departing Sydney.

item.title: Lululemon fined more than $700,000 by ACMA for spam email breaches
item.description: The Australian Communications and Media Authority says activewear fashion brand Lululemon sent more than 370,000 emails containing marketing content without an unsubscribe option.
